title: C. neoformans HMW DNA Extraction
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cmsrp","text":"High molecular weight gDNA extraction protocol for C. neoformans cycling cultures. Suitable for downstream sequencing and PCR applications. Should yield 10-40µg gDNA per 50mL culture. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":51,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"daotl","text":"Wash Buffer: 500mM NaCl, 50mM EDTA","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1cavg","text":"Spheroplasting Buffer: 1M sorbitol, 100mM Sodium Citrate (pH5.8), 10mM EDTA, 20mg/mL Sigma Lysing Enzyme (L1412, to be added immediately before use). ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7cot7","text":"Lysis Buffer: 100mM Tris (pH 7.5), 500mM NaCl, 100mM EDTA, 40µg/mL RNase A (to be added immediately before use).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e1foj","text":"2.5M KOAc in TE Buffer. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8trfk","text":"100% EtOH (Ice cold in freezer).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"64itc","text":"70% EtOH.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7liu8","text":"Tris pH 8.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fdamb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Grow approx 50mL C. neoformans culture to OD 1.5-1.7.
Centrifuge 2000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes , remove supernatant.
Wash three times with 3mL wash buffer (0.5M NaCl, 50mM EDTA).
Centrifuge as above, remove supernatent and flash freeze in liquid nitrogen or dry-ice.
Thaw pellet at 37 °C , wash once in dH 2 O and centrifuge 2000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Resuspend in 3mL sheroplasting buffer (1M sorbitol, 100mM Sodium Citrate (pH5.8), 10mM EDTA, 20mg/mL Sigma Lysing Enzyme (L1412)). Incubate in shaker 200 rpm, 37 °C, 3 hours
Centrifuge as above, remove supernatent and flash freeze in liquid nitrogen or dry-ice.
Thaw pellet at 37 °C , resuspend in 1.35mL lysis buffer (100mM Tris (pH 7.5), 500mM NaCl, 100mM EDTA, 40µg/mL RNase A), add 150µL 10% SDS. Incubate in shaker 200 rpm, 37 °C, 1 hour
Add 2mL 2.5M KOAc in TE, invert to mix, place in 15mL falcon tube and incubate On ice 00:05:00
Centrifuge 2000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes , and transfer supernatant to new 15mL falcon. Repeat twice .
Transfer to 50mL falcon tube (approx 2.5mL per sample), add 6.5mL ice-cold 100% EtOH. Invert gently to mix and incubate On ice 01:00:00, at least.
Spit sample into 6x 1.5mL eppendorf tubes (should just fit), spin 14000 rpm, 4 °C, 30 minutes to pellet DNA.
Carefully remove supernatant and add 500µL 70% EtOH to remove salts from DNA, do not disturb pellet. Spin 14000 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes .

Carefully remove supernatant (from sides of tube too, two rounds of aspiration can help with this) and allow pellet to dry 00:05:00 at RT, longer times may result in dry pellet that is difficult to resuspent.

Add 20µL Tris pH 8 to each tube and incubate at least 00:30:00 to allow pellet to dissolve (can also pipette up and down gently, but I allow this time to let the pellet dissolve slowly). Gently pipette up and down to finish dissolving, and aliquot six samples back to 1 tube (120µL total).
Spin 14000 rpm, Room temperature, 30 minutes to pellet any residual debris, and move supernatant to fresh eppendorf (low DNA binding tube ideally).
Quantify DNA content via nanodrop and/or Qbit.
Nanodrop 260/280 ratio of 2.0 and 260/230 of 2.2 expected for high quality gDNA. Concentration quatification may be high compared to Qbit, which will provide the more accurate figure.
Can repeat once to increase purity.
creator: Thomas Davies
